pattern: A level-order traversal that, on every odd depth, swaps the collected values symmetrically from the outside in reverses exactly those rows while leaving the tree's shape intact.
hints: The tree is perfect, so each level is completely filled and its nodes can be gathered left to right.
Only odd-depth levels change, and the change is reversing the sequence of values on that level.
Walk the tree breadth-first; whenever the current depth is odd, mirror the values across the level by swapping the kth node with the kth-from-last.

edge_inputs.description: single node has no odd level
symmetric values still swap by position
all equal values unchanged in appearance

stress.description: random perfect binary tree of a random full height
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    h = rng.randint(1, 8)
    count = (1 << h) - 1
    arr = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(count)]
    return [arr]
stress.note: perfect tree has 2^h - 1 nodes, matching the problem guarantee
